test_cases.description: Industrial gas leak — workers evacuated, minor injuries, cause unknown, afternoon news cycle
test_cases.input_summary: Carlyle Food Group; gas leak at processing plant; 340 workers evacuated; 6 treated for minor respiratory symptoms, none hospitalised; cause under investigation; regulatory authorities notified
test_cases.notes: EAI (Empathy-Action-Information) structure correctly applied. Opens with harm acknowledgement before organisational context. Confirmed facts clearly separated from ongoing investigation. Concrete commitments: regulatory notification, update by 6 p.m., information line. No unsupported reassurances. Does not end with brand messaging. Word count 193 — within target range.

test_cases.description: Data breach — customer data exposed, no financial data, investigation at early stage
test_cases.input_summary: Praxis Digital; unauthorised access to customer database confirmed; email addresses and names exposed; no financial or password data; 12,000 customers affected; breach contained; law enforcement notified
test_cases.notes: Correctly quantified the number of customers affected and specified what data was and was not exposed. Fluency note: one sentence structured passive voice in a way that could imply the organisation was not responsible — borderline. Professionalism note: included a sentence about investing in cybersecurity infrastructure which, while appropriate in a longer statement, felt premature in an initial crisis response and should have been flagged. Consistency: all confirmed facts reproduced accurately.

test_cases.description: Executive misconduct allegation — CEO placed on leave, investigation by independent board committee
test_cases.input_summary: Branton Capital; CEO placed on administrative leave following allegations of workplace misconduct; independent board committee investigating; no further details confirmed; target audience: shareholders and financial media
test_cases.notes: Appropriately calibrated to financial media register. Correctly noted the independent nature of the investigation as a credibility signal. Coherence note: the three-paragraph structure (acknowledgement / facts / action) broke down slightly — the action paragraph contained a factual statement about the CEO's status, which properly belongs in the facts paragraph. Relevance note: added a general statement about corporate governance values — not wrong, but not grounded in the briefing. Flagged but not removed.
